warnings: SAP session variables like $session.client cannot be directly mapped to Snowflake - using placeholders
ABAP-specific functions like dats_days_between, unit_conversion, fltp_to_dec have no direct Snowflake equivalents - approximated with standard functions
ABAP typed literals (abap.int4, abap.char, etc.) converted to standard SQL literals
Some fields referenced (seatsocc_b, seatsocc_f) may not exist in source table - assumed to be valid columns
ABAP 'IS NOT INITIAL' converted to 'IS NOT NULL' which may have different semantics for empty strings
Parameter handling differs between ABAP CDS and Snowflake views - parameters become view parameters
ABAP regex function replace_regexpr has different syntax than Snowflake REGEXP_REPLACE
Unit conversion from miles to kilometers approximated with multiplication factor
assumptions: Source table zdemo_abap_fli exists in Snowflake with same column structure
Columns seatsocc_b and seatsocc_f exist in the source table
Parameter p_smax will be passed as view parameter
ABAP semantic annotations are not preserved in Snowflake view
Client handling is not required (original had @AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #NOT_REQUIRED)
String functions behavior matches between ABAP and Snowflake for trailing blanks handling
conversion_notes: Converted ABAP CDS view entity to Snowflake parameterized view
Mapped ABAP built-in functions to closest Snowflake equivalents
Preserved all arithmetic, case, and logical expressions
Maintained WHERE clause filtering logic with parameter
Converted ABAP session variables to Snowflake system functions where possible
Approximated SAP-specific date/time functions with standard SQL functions
Removed SAP-specific annotations as they have no Snowflake equivalent
Maintained key field semantics through comments (Snowflake views don't have formal keys)
